head.hi: Memorandum by the Assistant Secretary of State for European Affairs (Merchant) to the Under Secretary of State (Smith)
head.note: Drafted by Deputy Assistant Secretary of State for European Affairs C. Burke Elbrick.

div.p: Ambassador Dillon’s cable no. 3623 of March 31 from ParisSupra. confirms his conclusion of last summer that French confidence in American leadership has continued to decline ever since the beginning of 1953. The cable supplements Embassy despatches concerning the rising tide of French concern over “McCarthyism” in the United States.

The Secretary was disturbed by this cable and has indicated his interest in referring it to Mr. Streibert and possibly the OCB to consider action which might be taken to correct the impression which Ambassador Dillon reports has been created in France. You will recall that the answers elicited last year by the Department’s “Eyes Only” circular telegram no. 53 of June 23 were considered and noted by the NSC. Although the matter was not referred to the OCB last year, I am informed that reference of the present cable to the OCB would not call for any further action on the part of the NSC. Accordingly, it is recommended that you distribute to the members of the OCB at the next luncheon meeting copies of the cable in question together with a covering memorandum. In addition to soliciting the views of the members of the OCB as to possible action, it is particularly recommended that Mr. Cutler’s views be sought in view of past consideration of this subject by the NSC.
